as_of_note: Verified against official docs as fetched on the dates below. Docs change.
notes: Headline platform: capped at needs_human. Named-on-a-slide claims require human sign-off, not tool self-certification.
Additionally, 3 required step(s) are not backed by a quoted official-doc excerpt.

steps.action: Open Vercel Documentation and follow Getting started.
steps.success_signal: The guide heading and prerequisites are visible.
notes: No literal official-doc excerpt supports the step's success_signal.

evidence.excerpt: Vercel Agent v0 ↗ Vercel MCP AI Integrations Deploy & scale Deployments CLI Functions Delivery Network Storage Integrations Microfrontends Domains Operate & protect Firewall Observability Feature Flags Toolbar Bot Management BotID Deployment Protection Compliance Learn Resources Changelog ↗ Blog ↗ Community ↗ Knowledge Base APIs & SDKs Templates Getting Started

steps.action: Create or obtain access to a Vercel account.
steps.success_signal: The account can authenticate through vercel login.

evidence.excerpt: Vercel Set up environment variables Add a custom domain Explore supported frameworks Vercel Functions – Run server-side code on demand Storage on Vercel Marketplace – Provision Postgres, Redis, NoSQL, and more with vercel install Connect the Vercel MCP server – Give AI agents direct access to your Vercel account

account Node.js 18+ Install the Vercel CLI Every Vercel workflow starts with the CLI. Install it whether or not you use an AI coding agent. Agents that can run terminal commands use the CLI to deploy, pull environment variables, and manage projects. Install Vercel CLI Terminal pnpm i - g vercel Log in to Vercel vercel login Follow the prompts to authenticate

Vercel Copy page Copy page Deploy your app on Vercel in three steps: install the CLI, add agent support if you use an AI coding agent, and deploy. AI Assistance Help me get set up with Vercel. Based on my project, do the following: 1. Install the Vercel CLI globally

Vercel CLI Terminal pnpm i - g vercel Log in to Vercel vercel login Follow the prompts to authenticate with your Vercel account. Deploy your project Navigate to your project directory and run: vercel The CLI detects your framework, builds your project, and deploys it. To deploy to production: vercel --prod See the CLI documentation for the full command

steps.action: Return the Deployment URL on standard output.
steps.success_signal: A deployment URL is present on stdout, completing the guide's deploy step.

evidence.excerpt: Output API for more details. Standard output usage When deploying, stdout is always the Deployment URL

deployment URL. Note that you may need to define the scope when using vercel alias deployDomain.sh # save stdout and stderr to files vercel deploy > deployment-url.txt 2> error.txt # check the exit code code = $? if [ $code -eq 0 ]; then # Now you can use the deployment url from stdout for the next step
